article_title: LIGO-Virgo-KAGRA Collaboration Releases GWTC-4, More Than Doubling the Catalog of Known Gravitational Wave Events to 218

findings.message: This article was requested by a human editor — apply heightened scrutiny to content accuracy and source quality

content_preview.title: LIGO-Virgo-KAGRA Collaboration Releases GWTC-4, More Than Doubling the Catalog of Known Gravitational Wave Events to 218
content_preview.summary: The fourth gravitational wave transient catalog adds 128 new detections from a nine-month observing run, including the heaviest and fastest-spinning black hole mergers ever recorded.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The LIGO-Virgo-KAGRA (LVK) Collaboration published the Gravitational-Wave Transient Catalog 4.0 (GWTC-4) on March 5, 2026, adding 128 new gravitational wave candidates detected during the observatories' fourth observing run. The release more than doubles the 90 events cataloged across the collaboration's three prior observing runs, according to [MIT News](https://news.mit.edu/2026/new-catalog-doubles-gravitational-wave-detections-made-ligo-virgo-kagra-0305), and includes the heavies...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt
1 source(s) blocked bot access — sources likely valid but not archivable automatically

editor_notes.content_quality: Excellent astrophysics news with clear structure. Notable events (GW231123, GW231028, GW231118, GW230814) are individually described with specific data. Good context linking to prior GW250114 article. Hubble constant discussion adds valuable cosmological context.
editor_notes.source_verification: MIT News (fetched): Confirms 128 candidates, 90 prior events, May 2023-January 2024 window, GW231123 ~130 solar masses each, GW231028 ~40% speed of light spin, GW231118 asymmetric pair, GW230814 relativity test, Vitale quote, Astrophysical Journal Letters. LIGO Caltech (fetched): Confirms all detector facilities (Hanford, Livingston, Virgo, KAGRA), ~300 total merger signals, Hubble constant 76 km/s/Mpc, March 5 2026 release. Caltech (fetched via WebFetch despite 403 on bot check): Confirms GW231123 130 solar masses, hierarchical merger explanation, catalog doubling.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All key claims verified across multiple sources. Event designations, masses, spin rates, Hubble constant value, detector locations, observing window, and Vitale quote all confirmed. The Vitale quote in the article ('the theory is passing all our tests') is consistent with MIT News source content. Total catalog count of 218 (90+128) is arithmetically correct.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. Analysis section appropriately contextualizes the field's maturation without editorializing.
editor_notes.originality: Prior GW250114 article (2026-02-05) covered a single event. This GWTC-4 catalog article is a distinct, complementary topic with no overlap. Internal link used appropriately.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality human-requested astrophysics submission with thorough source verification. All three sources confirm key claims. Ready for publication.
